sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club have struggled recently, winning only 1 of their last 5 matches with a tendency to concede late goals, as seen in losses like 2-3 to Sporting CP. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, are in fine form with 4 wins in their last 5 games, showing resilience with late winners and a strong attacking output. Their unbeaten streak of 7 matches gives them a clear edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these Basque rivals, Real Sociedad have had the upper hand, winning 3-2 in their most recent clash on November 1, 2025, with a dramatic late goal. The earlier match in May 2025 ended in a goalless draw, suggesting a tight contest, but Sociedad's recent attacking flair could tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Athletic Club, Gorka Guruzeta has been in form with 4 goals in his last 5 matches, while Nico Williams remains a threat with his dribbling (2.5 per game, 4th in the league). Real Sociedad’s Mikel Oyarzabal (8 goals this season) and Gonçalo Guedes (5 goals, recent standout in head-to-heads) will be crucial in breaking down Athletic’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Athletic Club excel in final third entries (1st in the league) and interceptions (2nd), indicating a high-pressing game, but their defense has been leaky with no clean sheet in 8 matches. Real Sociedad’s ability to score in multiple periods and consistent goal threat (both teams scoring in their last 8 games) suggests they can exploit Athletic’s defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Athletic Club have overperformed their expected goals by nearly 10 (2nd in the league), but their 30 goals conceded (13th) highlight defensive issues. Real Sociedad, with 29 goals scored and conceded, show balance, and their higher league position (8th vs. 14th) reflects better consistency.

sections.subtitle: Prediction Rationale
sections.text: Considering Real Sociedad’s superior form, head-to-head edge, and Athletic Club’s defensive struggles, the Sofascore model’s 42% probability for an away win seems justified. Expect a competitive match with goals on both sides, but Sociedad’s momentum should see them through.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Real Sociedad leads Athletic Club 1-0 at San Mamés in Bilbao. The first half saw Real Sociedad capitalize on their chances with a goal from Gonçalo Guedes in the 37th minute. Athletic Club, despite holding a slight edge in ball possession and showing intent, couldn’t convert their efforts into goals. The home side struggled to create clear-cut opportunities, while the visitors were more clinical despite having fewer entries into the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Athletic Club's performance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club has shown resilience in maintaining possession and pushing forward, with frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. However, their inability to register shots on target is a glaring issue. Players like Álex Berenguer and Gorka Guruzeta have shown some promise in attack but need to be more decisive. Defensively, they’ve held up reasonably well, with players like Adama Boiro and Aitor Paredes making key contributions. They’ll need to sharpen their finishing if they hope to turn this game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Sociedad's performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad has been more effective in front of goal, with Gonçalo Guedes leading the charge by scoring and posing a constant threat. Their attacking play has been efficient, creating big chances despite fewer forays into the final third compared to Athletic Club. Defensively, they’ve managed to keep Athletic at bay, with players like Igor Zubeldia and Jon Gorrotxategi stepping up. However, a single yellow card for Zubeldia signals some disciplinary concerns that could be exploited if Athletic ramps up the pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Athletic Club showing early dominance, dictating the tempo with consistent attacking spells for much of the opening 20 minutes. Real Sociedad, however, gradually shifted the momentum in their favor with a significant surge in attacking intent around the midway point. This culminated in a strong push leading up to their goal, after which they maintained control for the remainder of the half. Athletic Club struggled to regain their earlier rhythm, with only fleeting moments of threat as the whistle approached.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Athletic Club is likely to come out with renewed vigor, especially playing at home. They’ll need to convert their final third presence into tangible chances, potentially relying on players like Nico Williams to unlock Real Sociedad’s defense. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, will aim to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities through Guedes, who has been their standout performer. The game could open up if Athletic pushes hard, but Real Sociedad’s clinical edge might prove decisive.
